title: Recall sum-to-product applications
text: When are sum-to-product formulas most commonly used in trigonometry?

hintAnswer: It allows factoring, which is useful for solving equations or finding zeros.
title: Identify primary use
text: What is a key advantage of transforming a sum of trigonometric functions into a product?
choices: It allows factoring, which is useful for solving equations or finding zeros.
It converts all trigonometric functions to sine and cosine.
It reduces the number of terms in an expression.
It always simplifies the amplitude of the resulting function.

title: Consider solving equations
text: Think about how factoring helps solve algebraic equations. Can sum-to-product formulas achieve a similar effect for trigonometric equations?

title: Formulate an example
text: Can you create a simple trigonometric equation involving a sum of sines or cosines that would be easier to solve after applying a sum-to-product formula?
